title: Every Call Is the Only Call That Matters
tagline: A patient crashing mid-transport, twelve minutes from the hospital, and a decision that can't wait for the ER.

wake_up.text: Twelve-hour shift, ambulance already checked over by the night crew. Your partner hands you a coffee before you've even asked.

choices.text: Do your own full check of the rig anyway
Trust the night crew's handoff and get moving

shift_briefing.text: Dispatch briefing: a busy day expected, a marathon downtown adding road closures to account for on every call.

choices.text: Study the road closure map in detail before rolling out
Figure out routes as you go

first_call.text: First call: an elderly fall, minor injuries, mostly frightened. Routine, but never quite nothing.

choices.text: Take the extra time to reassure her properly
Keep it efficient — there's a full board today

random_moment.text: Between calls, the morning fills in with the small, unscripted moments that make up most of any shift.

choices.text: A grateful family from yesterday's call flags you down to say thanks
Dispatch radio cuts out with static for a tense minute
You catch an easy parking spot on a call that's usually a nightmare for access

second_call.text: A chest pain call, moderate risk. The patient is anxious and talking fast, which is making his vitals worse by the minute.

choices.text: Spend the extra minute calming him down before moving
Load and go — time to the hospital matters more right now

transport_start.text: Transport underway, twelve minutes to the hospital under normal traffic. His vitals are holding, for now.

choices.text: Monitor closely en route

patient_crashes.text: Six minutes in, his heart rhythm changes fast — he's crashing, right now, in the back of a moving ambulance, and the hospital is still eight minutes out at best.

choices.text: Perform the intervention yourself, right now, in the moving rig
Have your partner divert to the closer, smaller hospital instead
Call medical direction for real-time guidance before acting
Continue to the original hospital as planned, stabilizing en route

crash_outcome_intervene.text: Hands steady despite the moving rig, the intervention works. By the time you reach the ER, he's stable enough that the trauma team barely has to rush.

choices.text: Hand off to the ER team

crash_outcome_divert.text: The smaller hospital isn't the destination you planned, but it's four minutes closer, and four minutes turns out to be exactly the margin that mattered.

choices.text: Hand off to the ER team

crash_outcome_direction.text: Medical direction walks you through it over the radio, calm and precise. It costs a few seconds getting the call connected — seconds that, this time, you had.

choices.text: Hand off to the ER team

crash_outcome_continue.text: He holds on for the full eight minutes to the original hospital, barely, and the ER team is waiting at the door when you arrive — but it was closer than anyone in the back of that rig would like to admit.

choices.text: Hand off to the ER team

afternoon_lull.text: A rare quiet stretch. The rig's restocked, the paperwork from this morning is caught up, mostly.

choices.text: Actually sit down and eat lunch
Catch up on documentation instead

afternoon_call.text: A call near the marathon route — a runner down, heat exhaustion, in a crowd that's making access difficult.

choices.text: Push directly through the crowd to reach them fastest
Request police crowd control before moving in

difficult_family.text: A call to a home where a family member is combative and frightened, making it hard to actually help the patient.

choices.text: Take the time to de-escalate the situation before treating
Focus on the patient and work around the tension

evening_call.text: A pediatric call — always the hardest kind, no matter how many years you've done this.

choices.text: Stay steady and fully present, however hard it is
Keep it efficient and clinical to get through it

end_of_shift_paperwork.text: Documentation for the day's calls, the part of the job nobody puts in the recruiting brochure.

choices.text: Write thorough, careful notes on every call
Keep the notes brief and get home

end_shift.text: Rig restocked for the next crew. Whatever tonight brings for someone else, it isn't yours anymore.

choices.text: Head home

triumphant.title: The Call You'll Remember
triumphant.blurb: Every decision today landed right, including the one that mattered most. This is the shift that reminds you why you do this.

steady_hand.title: Solid Shift
steady_hand.blurb: No headlines, no hesitation — just careful, competent care on every single call, all day long.

ordinary_day.title: A Full Board
ordinary_day.blurb: A busy, unremarkable shift by the job's strange standard. Everyone you touched today is better off for it.

burned_out.title: Running on Fumes
burned_out.blurb: You made it to the end of the shift, but you're not sure how. Call after call, this pace has a real cost.

written_up.title: Quality Review
written_up.blurb: A few of today's calls are heading into a formal quality review. Everyone's safe. The service still wants to understand the choices.
